q: Audible means able to be ___.
___ means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of frequencies.
The human's ear is capable of detecting sounds only in a ___ range of frequencies.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit is $20 Hz$ and the upper limit is about ___ $Hz$.
These limits are known as the ___ of audibility.
By using ___ generator, the audible frequency range can be determined.
The sonar is a type of technology that works based on ___.
___ stands for sound navigation and ranging.
Ships use sonar for ___ at sea.
Sonar can detect the position of other ___.
The depth of the sea can be found by sending out a ___ (a pulse of sound).
A signal is sent out from a ___ (transmitter) in sonar.
The time interval for the ___ signal (the echo) to be received is noted in sonar.
___ waves ($f < 20 Hz$) have long wavelengths.
Infrasound waves are able to travel over ___ distances.
Elephants communicate over a long distance using frequencies as low as ___ $Hz$.
A ___ uses frequencies down to $10 Hz$.
Infrasound waves travel longer distances with less ___.
Frequencies used in imaging are typically in the range of $1$ to ___ $MHz$.
A dog's hearing range goes up to ___ $Hz$.
A bat's hearing range starts from ___ $Hz$.
A dolphin's hearing range goes up to ___ $Hz$.
A cat's hearing range goes up to ___ $Hz$.
Humans' voicing range is $85$-$1,100$ ___.
The upper limit of audibility can be recorded by increasing sound frequency up to about ___ $kHz$.
Sonar can find the position of the shoals of ___.
